name: District of Columbia (United States)

colors.symbolism: the field of the Washington family arms
the bars and stars from the Washington family coat of arms

description: A white field charged with three red five-pointed stars in a horizontal row across the upper portion, above two horizontal red bars (a wide stripe and a narrower stripe, separated by white) running across the lower half.
history: Designed by Charles A. R. Dunn in 1924 and adopted on 15 October 1938 by act of the United States Congress. The design is a direct banner-of-arms rendering of the coat of arms of the Washington family, the ancestors of George Washington, granted in the 14th century.

features.symbolism: from the Washington family arms; sometimes interpreted as honoring George Washington.
from the Washington family arms; the entire design is a banner of arms based on the shield of George Washington's ancestors.
